exemplar.title: To what extent Intelâs recycling practices help to reduce electronic waste while exploring methods that they can adopt?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent Intelâs recycling practices help to reduce electronic waste while exploring methods that they can adopt?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Sustainability is central throughout, with the triple bottom line used to structure analysis
Clear connection to Intelâs waste-to-landfill targets and comparative AMD data
Conclusion returns to long-term environmental goals, framing strategic implications
weaknesses: Some passages remain descriptive rather than analytical
Depth of integration varies across sections
Introduction could more explicitly justify choice of sustainability as key concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the IA maintains sustainability at its core and demonstrates good analytical thinking, several sections revert to description without deeper integration. The rubric descriptor for band 4 requires strong, partially integrated analysis throughout, but uneven depth and occasional narrative recitals more closely align with band 3. Therefore, a score of 3 reflects solid understanding with room to strengthen analytical integration.

strengths: Four supporting documents provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement
All documents are relevant and furnish useful statistics and context
Footnote citations effectively bolster the circular economy discussion
weaknesses: Depth of engagement is unevenâsome sources contribute only one data point
Opportunities exist to draw broader insights from each document
Greater diversity of document types could enhance depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student supplied an adequate number of relevant, in-depth documents that inform the analysis, matching the band 3 descriptor. Although coverage is sufficient, uneven exploitation of sources prevents a higher award.

strengths: Triple Bottom Line tool is applied to judge Intelâs social, environmental, and economic impacts
The AMD comparison heading effectively frames competitor analysis
weaknesses: Only one principal analytical tool is selectedâlimited theoretical variety
Application sometimes assumes links (e.g., cost savings) without supporting data
Methodological steps lack clear structure and transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools is restricted to a single framework, fitting the rubricâs band 1 for limited selection. Though application is sometimes adequate, the narrow toolset constrains overall performance, warranting a score of 1.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Analysis moves beyond description via benchmarks (AMD, Brazil) and multi-dimensional evaluation
Multiple quantitative data sets are integrated into the narrative
Some evaluative comments link data trends to sustainability implications
weaknesses: Critical depth is inconsistentâlacks feasibility critique and resource-requirement analysis
Scant critical interpretation of certain figures and trend implications
Limitations section is too general about data scarcity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation offers adequate data selection and some analysis of the research question, consistent with band 3. However, inconsistent depth and partial evaluative integration prevent attainment of higher bands.

strengths: Conclusions explicitly answer the research question in a balanced manner
Key evidence (e.g., recycling rates) is referenced to support judgments
Recommendations on technology investment look forward to further improvement
weaknesses: Occasional assertions (cost savings) are not fully evidenced
Conclusion does not explicitly echo the exact research-question wording
Recommendations could be more tightly linked to analyzed data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are generally consistent with evidence and directly address the research question, fitting band 2 for partial consistency. Some unsupported assertions and weaker linkage to exact RQ wording preclude band 3.

strengths: Logical organization with clear headings guiding the reader
Contains all structural components (title page, TOC, intro, analysis, conclusion)
âTriple bottom line analysisâ heading effectively signals key focus
weaknesses: Table of contents is overly long and visually cluttered
Minor disorganization in sentence breaks and stray citation markers
Introductionâs final sentence is incomplete, reducing clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate, coherent structure throughout, meriting full marks (band 2). Although minor disorganization exists, the overall organization meets rubric expectations for this level.

strengths: All required elements are present: title page, RQ, key concept, word count, citations, bibliography
Figures and supporting documents are labelled
In-text citations are consistently used
weaknesses: Figures lack axis labels, units and descriptive captions
Contents page layout could be more professional (alignment, line breaks)
Some captions do not clearly summarize key takeaways
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission includes all mandated IA elements and demonstrates competent presentation, aligning with the band 2 descriptor. Minor layout and caption issues prevent perfection but do not detract from full-credit compliance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structureâtitle page, contents, introduction, analysis sections, conclusion, bibliography, appendicesâand headings guide the reader.  Minor disorganisation (very long table of contents artefact, broken sentences) prevents a higher award, but overall the structure is appropriate, meriting the highest available mark in this criterion.

All four documents are clearly relevant (Intel CSR, Brazilian e-waste study, AMD CSR, AMD data tables) and provide statistics, context, and comparative insight.  While coverage is appropriate, depth is unevenâsome documents are mined only for a single figureâso the evidence is âsufficiently in-depthâ rather than extensive.  This aligns with band 3.

The submission includes all required IA elements: title page with RQ, key concept, word count; table of contents; in-text citations; bibliography; and labelled supporting documents.  Hence it fulfils the criteria for complete presentation.
Four supporting documents are supplied, falling inside the required 3-5 range.  This matches the rubric band for adequate document quantity.

The triple bottom line is applied directly to Intelâs recycling practices and used to judge social, environmental and economic impacts; comparison with AMD further grounds the tool in the research question.  At times the link between evidence and interpretation is loose (e.g., profit discussion assumes cost savings), but overall the application is adequate and relevant, matching band 3.

The student selects multiple quantitative data sets (Intel hazardous waste trend, landfill percentages, AMD diversion rates, Brazil recycling volume) and integrates them into the narrative.  Although the range is not exhaustive and some figures are only described, the selection and use are clearly âadequateâ, consistent with band 3.

Analysis moves beyond description by comparing Intelâs performance with benchmarks (AMD, Brazil) and by evaluating social, environmental and profit dimensions.  However, evaluative depth is inconsistent and integration of different analytical strands is only partial.  This constitutes âsome analysis of the research questionâ, placing it in band 3.

The final paragraph explicitly answers âto what extentâ by acknowledging Intelâs progress yet highlighting remaining inefficiencies and recommending technology investment.  This direct, balanced statement constitutes an explicit answer to the research question, satisfying the top band for this strand.

The investigation relies almost exclusively on one analytical tool, the Triple Bottom Line, supplemented by basic descriptive statistics.  The limited variety places the work in the âsome tools and theories selectedâ category, commensurate with band 2.

Conclusions draw on earlier discussionâciting the 3.6 % Brazilian recycling rate and 63 % Intel upcycling figureâbut occasionally introduce assertions (e.g., cost savings from Recicla PC) that are not fully evidenced in the analysis.  Therefore the consistency with evidence is partial, fitting band 2.

Intel is consistently at the core of the discussion: corporate-level statistics, CSR targets, and specific partnerships (Recicla PC, Dell collaboration) are examined.  Comparative material on AMD is used primarily as a benchmark to evaluate Intel, reinforcingârather than distracting fromâthe focus on the chosen organisation.  The link to Intelâs strategic position is analysed (e.g., risk to reputation if Brazilian recycling stays at 3.6 %), but again some sections lapse into narrative description.  This represents strong, partially integrated organisational analysis rather than a fully seamless integration.

The student keeps the key concept of sustainability at the centre of the whole IA.  The introduction frames the research around Intelâs waste-to-landfill target, the analysis section repeatedly interprets data through a sustainability lens, the triple bottom line is explicitly used and the conclusion returns to long-term environmental goals.  Although references to sustainability appear in every section, the depth of integration varies: some passages are descriptive (e.g., recital of Intelâs 63 % upcycling rate) without pushing the analytical discussion further.  Because the concept is clearly understood and frequently but not flawlessly woven into analysis, the work fits the descriptor of a âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysis.â

annotations.comment: Labeling the key concept as sustainability is appropriate, but consider briefly justifying its relevance in the introduction to strengthen conceptual integration.
Embedding the methodology within a quotation reduces clarity. Outline each method step in a structured list for transparency.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The section heading repeats âAnalysis Current recycling practices at Intelââconsider simplifying to âCurrent Recycling Practicesâ for brevity.
The description of Intelâs circular economy goals lists activities but lacks critique of feasibility or resource requirements.
Figure 2 should include axis labels and a concise analytical caption explaining trends over time.
Your footnote citation is relevant and supports the circular economy discussion; keep this level of documentation.
The description of partnerships with Circular Brain and Dell is detailed but lacks critical evaluation of social, environmental or financial outcomes.
There is a stray citation marker (âp.2).â) that interrupts narrative flow; integrate it properly or move to footnote.
Including the figure caption 'AMD environmental data tables' helps orient the readerâgood presentation practice.
The research question is clear and focused, but it could be refined to specify metrics or criteria (e.g., reduction percentage targets) to guide analysis more precisely.
The table of contents is overly long and visually cluttered, which hinders navigation. Simplify entries and remove excessive dots for clarity.
Consider aligning page numbers flush right and removing line breaks within items to improve the professional layout of the contents page.
The opening history of Intel is descriptive but does not yet connect to the sustainability focus. Shift emphasis toward environmental impacts from the outset.
The statistic on hazardous waste doubling since 2015 is relevant, but the narrative lacks critical interpretation of trends or implications for sustainability.
Figure 1 lacks axis labels, units and a descriptive caption that explains key takeaways. Include these to improve reader comprehension.
The final sentence of the introduction is incomplete and ambiguous. Restate the full research question and outline specific research objectives.
Your claim that Dell partnership raises awareness needs evidence (e.g., survey metrics) to substantiate impact on consumer behaviour.
The critique of Brazilâs 3.6% recycling rate introduces reputational riskâexpand this with quantitative risk assessment or scenario analysis.
The figure illustrating Brazilâs recycling performance is useful but missing a clear title and key takeaway in the caption.
The âTriple bottom line analysisâ heading clearly signals analytical focusâgood structural choice.
The environmental factor integrates data well (63% upcycling) but could analyze trade-offs between reuse and potential resource constraints.
The profit argument hinges on consumer participation data that is not provided; include participation rates or costâbenefit estimates.
The AMD comparison heading effectively frames competitor analysisâthis strengthens relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The decline from 85% to 63% recycling at AMD is noted but not critically compared to Intelâs trend; draw a clearer evaluative parallel.
The AMD environmental data table is dense; summarize key trends in text rather than reproducing every site figure.
The triple bottom line recap in the conclusion is somewhat repetitive; instead, synthesize insights into an integrated evaluative summary.
The limitations section identifies data scarcity but is too general; specify which data were unavailable and how this affects reliability.
Methodological recommendations drawn from AMD practices are interesting, but clarify how these tie back to the research questionâs scope.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The conclusion restates key findings but should explicitly link back to the exact wording of the research question.
The final recommendation on technology investment is sound but needs direct linkage to the evidence presented (e.g., AMD case study).
